assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states PDBs are the most common cause and mentions '90% chance this is your issue' and 'Most likely scenario: You have a PDB with minAvailable set too high'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' and additional commands to check PDB details and find problematic ones

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check bare pods, terminating pods, and provides detailed PDB analysis commands to identify blocking pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check for pending pods and FailedScheduling events, plus mentions resource constraints as a potential issue

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl patch commands to relax PDBs by adjusting maxUnavailable and minAvailable settings

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud commands to increase max-surge-upgrade and adjust max-unavailable-upgrade settings

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: The response does not mention admission webhooks or validating/mutating webhooks as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Includes a 'Monitor Progress' section with commands to watch node upgrade progress, check operation status, and verify no stuck pods
